## eatmoreduck/boss-zhipin-scraper

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 661 В· Python В· 2026-07-12  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/eatmoreduck/boss-zhipin-scraper) В· [Website](https://blog.xiaohuangyu.space/p/boss-zhipin-scraper-open-source/)  
> `#Data Scraping` `#Job Analysis` `#Salary Parsing` `#boss-zhipin` `#boss-zhipin-scraper` `#boss-zhipin-spider` `#bosszp` `#chrome-cdp` `#crawler` `#job-scraper` `#python` `#scraper` `#spider` `#zhipin` 
> The Boss Zhipin Scraper is a job data extraction tool based on the Chrome DevTools Protocol, capable of reusing real login sessions to bypass frontend anti-scraping mechanisms and outputting job information with clear salary in JSON and CSV formats. It provides salary distribution, skill analysis, and generates optimization prompts for job materials, making it suitable for data analysis and job market research.
> <sub>BossзӣҙиҒҳзҲ¬иҷ« / BOSSзӣҙиҒҳиҒҢдҪҚж•°жҚ®жҠ“еҸ–е·Ҙе…·пјҢеҹәдәҺ Chrome CDP еҚҸи®®еӨҚз”Ёзңҹе®һзҷ»еҪ•жҖҒпјҢз»•иҝҮеӯ—дҪ“еҸҚзҲ¬пјҢиҫ“еҮәжҳҺж–Үи–Әиө„ JSON/CSV + и–Әиө„жҠҖиғҪеҲҶжһҗгҖӮA Chrome-CDP-based BOSS Zhipin job scraper/crawler.</sub>

## aatmaj28/FinRAG

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 2 В· HTML В· 2025-04-13  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/aatmaj28/FinRAG)  
> `#Retrieval-Augmented Intelligence` `#Financial Document Analysis` `#Multi-Model Support` 
> FinRAG is a Retrieval-Augmented Intelligence system designed for intelligent interaction with 10-K reports and financial documents. It features a pre-processed RAG system for querying an existing financial knowledge base and a Document Q&A system for users to upload and analyze their own financial documents. FinRAG combines semantic and keyword retrieval techniques, supports multiple AI models, and offers efficient text processing and company information integration, making it ideal for financial analysts and researchers.
> <sub>I introduce FinRAG: A Retrieval-Augmented Intelligence system designed for intelligent in- teraction with 10-K reports. FinRAG offers two key functionalities: a pre-processed RAG System for querying an existing financial knowledge base, and a Document Q&A system allowing users to upload and analyze their own financial documents.</sub>

## BoundaryML/baml

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 8,538 В· Rust В· 2026-07-14  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/BoundaryML/baml) В· [Website](https://boundaryml.com/explore)  
> `#Agent Programming` `#Static Typing` `#Runtime Types` `#boundaryml` `#guardrails` `#llm` `#programming-language` `#structured-data` 
> BAML is a programming language designed for agents, featuring static typing and runtime type persistence to minimize programming errors. Its syntax resembles TypeScript while incorporating Rust's type safety and Go's concurrency model. Users can run BAML standalone or interoperate with languages like Python and TypeScript, making it suitable for various agent development scenarios.
> <sub>The programming language for agents</sub>

> вӯҗ 495 В· JavaScript В· 2026-07-13  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/xiaoyuanda666-ship-it/BaiLongma)  
> `#Desktop AI Agent` `#Continuous Operation` `#Memory Management` 
> Bailongma is a continuously running desktop AI Agent capable of handling user messages, memory management, and task execution. It updates its status in real-time through a main loop and supports multiple language models and social platform connections. Built with Electron, it integrates features like speech recognition, dynamic context injection, and local resource awareness, aiming to deliver an intelligent and interactive desktop assistant experience.

This tool supports various document formats and requires no API key, making it ideal for efficient knowledge management and extraction.
> <sub>A personal knowledge base that builds and maintains itself. Drop in sources вҖ” Claude (or Codex/Gemini) reads them, extracts knowledge, and maintains a persistent interlinked wiki. Works with Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI. No API key needed.</sub>

## nashsu/llm_wiki

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 14,489 В· TypeScript В· 2026-07-11  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/nashsu/llm_wiki)  
> `#Knowledge Management` `#Incremental Building` `#Multimodal Processing` 
> LLM Wiki is a cross-platform desktop application that automatically transforms documents into an organized, interlinked knowledge base. Unlike traditional retrieve-and-answer methods, LLM Wiki incrementally builds and maintains a persistent wiki, ensuring continuous knowledge updates without re-deriving information. Based on Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern, it incorporates advanced features like multimodal image processing, knowledge graphs, and vector semantic search, making it ideal for personal knowledge management and information integration.
> <sub>LLM Wiki is a cross-platform desktop application that turns your documents into an organized, interlinked knowledge base вҖ” automatically. Instead of traditional RAG (retrieve-and-answer from scratch every time), the LLM incrementally builds and maintains a persistent wiki from your sourcesгҖӮ</sub>

> VoxCPM2 is a tokenizer-free Text-to-Speech system that utilizes an end-to-end diffusion autoregressive architecture to generate continuous speech representations. Supporting 30 languages, it features creative voice design and controllable voice cloning, producing audio at up to 48kHz quality. The latest version boasts 2 billion parameters trained on over 2 million hours of multilingual speech data, capable of generating entirely new voices from natural language descriptions and supporting real-time streaming, making it suitable for various creative speech generation applications.
> <sub>VoxCPM2: Tokenizer-Free TTS for Multilingual Speech Generation, Creative Voice Design, and True-to-Life Cloning</sub>

## TheTom/turboquant_plus

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 6,992 В· Python В· 2026-06-26  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/TheTom/turboquant_plus)  
> `#KV Cache Compression` `#Local Inference` `#Performance Optimization` 
> TurboQuant+ is an implementation of TurboQuant, focusing on enhancing local LLM inference efficiency through KV cache compression. The project offers experimental integrations that allow benchmarking and quality validation across different hardware and backends. Core technologies include PolarQuant and Walsh-Hadamard rotation, achieving 3.8-6.4x KV cache compression while maintaining near-equivalent decoding speeds in long contexts. Key findings indicate that V compression has negligible effects on attention quality, while K compression is the primary cause of quality degradation.

> llama.cpp is a high-performance C/C++ implementation designed to simplify large language model (LLM) inference across various hardware architectures. It offers a dependency-free implementation optimized for Apple Silicon and multiple x86 and RISC-V architectures, supporting various quantization methods for faster inference and reduced memory usage. The project includes two auditing skills and 11 reusable modules that can detect security issues such as prompt injection, supply chain attacks, and credential leaks. Users can load these modules through Codex CLI, Claude Code, or any LLM to ensure comprehensive security checks before installing and using skills.
> <sub>Curated, security-first OpenClaw skills (Markdown-based). Security audit skills - detect prompt injection, supply chain attacks, credential leaks. Works with Codex CLI, Claude Code, any LLM.</sub>

> MCO is a neutral orchestration layer that enables the simultaneous dispatch of multiple AI coding agents (such as Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.) with any prompt and IDE. Developers can assign tasks to multiple agents to execute in parallel, allowing for comparison of outputs and enhancing development efficiency. It is particularly useful in scenarios requiring multi-perspective analysis, especially in code reviews and task execution.
> <sub>[Successor: Hive вҖ” https://hivehq.dev] Original neutral orchestration layer for Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Qwen Code. New work continues in Hive.</sub>

Its parser learns from website changes, automatically relocating elements when pages update. Built-in fetchers bypass anti-bot systems like Cloudflare Turnstile effortlessly. It leverages 20,000 hours of real-world data from nine popular dual-arm robot configurations, demonstrating significant superiority in simulation and real-world benchmarks. The model achieves a 1.5 to 2.8 times speedup in training efficiency compared to existing VLA codebases, making it suitable for applications in robotic control and human-robot interaction.

> PageIndex is a vectorless, reasoning-based document indexing system designed to enhance retrieval accuracy for long documents. By constructing a hierarchical tree index, it simulates how human experts extract knowledge, enabling context-aware retrieval. This system is particularly suited for scenarios requiring domain expertise and multi-step reasoning, addressing the limitations of traditional vector databases in semantic similarity.

> AIClient2API is a powerful proxy that unifies requests from various client-only large model APIs, including Gemini CLI, Antigravity, Codex, Grok, and Kiro. It is compatible with the OpenAI API, supporting thousands of Gemini model requests per day and offering free access to the built-in Claude model in Kiro. Easily connect to any client via the API, enhancing AI development efficiency.
> <sub>Self-hosted multi-protocol AI API proxy for Antigravity, Codex, Grok, Kiro, OpenAI, Claude, and custom providers. Supports OpenAI-compatible API, Claude API, Gemini protocol conversion, GPT, Grok Build, Claude Opus, Gemini Pro, Kimi, MiniMax, provider pools, smart routing, and automatic failover.</sub>

> `#Context Design` `#Large Language Models` `#Optimization Techniques` 
> Context Engineering is a novel paradigm that transcends prompt engineering by precisely designing and optimizing contextual information to enhance the performance of large language models. This project offers a systematic review of research and practical tools to help developers understand and apply the core principles of context design, suitable for various applications like intelligent assistants and dialogue systems.
> <sub>"Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." вҖ” Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.</sub>

Unlike traditional retrieval-augmented generation methods, Graphiti continuously integrates user interactions, structured and unstructured enterprise data, and external information into a coherent, queryable graph. It supports incremental data updates, efficient retrieval, and precise historical queries, making it suitable for developing interactive, context-aware AI applications.
> <sub>Build Real-Time Knowledge Graphs for AI Agents</sub>

> `#Model Serving` `#Multimodal Inference` `#Auto Batching` `#artificial-intelligence` `#chatglm` `#deployment` `#flan-t5` `#gemma` `#ggml` `#glm4` `#inference` `#llama` `#llama3` `#llamacpp` `#llm` `#machine-learning` `#mistral` `#openai-api` `#pytorch` `#qwen` `#vllm` `#whisper` `#wizardlm` 
> Xorbits Inference (Xinference) is a powerful and versatile library designed to simplify the serving of language, speech recognition, and multimodal models. Users can effortlessly switch between GPT and other large language models with a single line of code, supporting deployment on cloud, on-prem, or personal laptops. Key highlights include auto-batching, distributed inference, and support for multiple backends, making it suitable for researchers, developers, and data scientists.
> <sub>Swap GPT for any LLM by changing a single line of code. Xinference lets you run open-source, speech, and multimodal models on cloud, on-prem, or your laptop вҖ” all through one unified, production-ready inference API.</sub>

> Vecto3d is a super simple tool designed to convert SVG graphics, primarily logos, into 3D models. It offers various customization options, including geometry, materials, and environment settings, allowing users to easily adjust model thickness and colors, and download 3D models in STL, GLB, and GLTF formats. Additionally, users can export high-quality images and experience a unique 'vibe mode'.
> <sub>A super simple tool to convert your SVG's to 3D models.</sub>

> RF-DETR is a real-time object detection and segmentation model architecture developed by Roboflow, built on a DINOv2 vision transformer backbone. It achieves state-of-the-art performance on COCO and RF100-VL datasets, offering excellent accuracy and latency trade-offs. The model supports both detection and instance segmentation through a consistent API, making it suitable for efficient real-time computer vision applications.
> <sub>RF-DETR is a real-time object detection and segmentation model architecture developed by Roboflow, SOTA on COCO, designed for fine-tuning. [ICLR 2026]</sub>

> Wav2Lip is an efficient lip-sync generation tool that accurately synchronizes lip movements with audio in complex environments, based on research published at ACM Multimedia 2020. The project offers an easy-to-use API interface, supporting both Python and TypeScript, allowing users to quickly generate synchronized videos by providing video and audio files, making it suitable for film production, game development, and more.
> <sub>This repository contains the codes of "A Lip Sync Expert Is All You Need for Speech to Lip Generation In the Wild", published at ACM Multimedia 2020. For HD commercial model, please try out Sync Labs</sub>

## python-poetry/poetry

> `#Dependency Management` `#Python Packaging` `#Project Configuration` `#dependency-manager` `#package-manager` `#packaging` `#poetry` `#python` 
> Poetry is a dependency management and packaging tool for Python projects, designed to simplify the declaration, management, and installation of dependencies. It replaces traditional files like `setup.py` and `requirements.txt` with a simple `pyproject.toml` format, ensuring correct project configuration across environments. Poetry also supports dependency groups and optional dependencies, making project management more flexible and efficient.
> <sub>Python packaging and dependency management made easy</sub>

> WrenAI is an open-source Generative Business Intelligence (GenBI) tool that enables users to query any database in natural language, generating accurate SQL statements and charts rapidly. Key features include multi-language query support, AI-generated reports and charts, and a semantic layer ensuring output accuracy. Ideal for businesses needing quick data analysis and decision support, it empowers users to easily gain data insights.
> <sub>GenBI (Generative BI) for AI agents, an open-source, governed text-to-SQL through an open context layer that turns natural-language questions into trusted dashboards, charts, and SQL across 20+ data sources, such as BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Amazon Redshift, Databricks and more.</sub>

## UCSC-VLAA/story-iter

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 960 В· Python В· 2026-04-02  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/UCSC-VLAA/story-iter) В· [Website](https://jwmao1.github.io/storyiter/)  
> `#Long Story Visualization` `#Training-free Framework` `#Global Reference Cross-Attention` `#diffusion-models` `#generative-art` `#generative-model` `#image-generation` `#storytelling` `#visual-storytelling` 
> Story-Iter is a training-free iterative framework designed to enhance the quality of long story visualization. By introducing a Global Reference Cross-Attention (GRCA) module, it progressively integrates all reference images from previous rounds, ensuring semantic consistency and fine-grained interactions. Unlike traditional methods relying on fixed reference images, Story-Iter achieves efficient and precise image generation in narratives of up to 100 frames, making it suitable for applications requiring complex storytelling.
> <sub>[ICLR 2026] A Training-free Iterative Framework for Long Story Visualization</sub>

> nvm-desktop is a desktop application designed to manage multiple Node.js versions through a visual interface. It supports macOS, Windows, and Linux, allowing users to easily install, manage, and switch between different Node.js versions independently of OS-specific features. The application provides project-level version isolation, ensuring global dependencies do not interfere with each other. Its smart version detection is powered by nvmd-command, a Rust-based executable, ensuring efficiency and reliability.

> EDTalk is an efficient framework for emotional talking head synthesis, aiming to achieve disentangled control over facial motions. The framework decomposes facial dynamics into three independent latent spacesвҖ”mouth shape, head pose, and emotional expressionвҖ”using lightweight modules, enabling personalized manipulation based on video and audio inputs. > MARS5 is a novel speech synthesis model from CAMB.AI, utilizing a unique two-stage AR-NAR architecture to generate high-quality speech from just 5 seconds of audio and a text snippet. It utilizes samples from the Common Voice and DiDiSpeech-2 datasets, employing word error rate (WER) and speaker similarity (SIM) as evaluation metrics, supporting both English and Mandarin speech synthesis and conversion tasks.

## BlinkDL/RWKV-LM

> вӯҗ 14,612 В· Python В· 2026-07-13  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/BlinkDL/RWKV-LM)  
> `#RNN` `#Large Language Model` `#Efficient Inference` `#attention-mechanism` `#chatgpt` `#deep-learning` `#gpt` `#gpt-2` `#gpt-3` `#language-model` `#linear-attention` `#lstm` `#pytorch` `#rnn` `#rwkv` `#transformer` `#transformers` 
> RWKV is an RNN with exceptional performance in large language models, combining the strengths of RNNs and Transformers with linear time and constant space characteristics, making it suitable for LLM and multimodal applications. RWKV-7 'Goose' supports fast training and infinite context length without kv-cache, offering efficient inference capabilities. The project is completely free, ideal for developers and researchers looking to upgrade models and develop applications.
> <sub>RWKV (pronounced RwaKuv) is an RNN with great LLM performance, which can also be directly trained like a GPT transformer (parallelizable). We are at RWKV-7 "Goose". So it's combining the best of RNN and transformer - great performance, linear time, constant space (no kv-cache), fast training, infinite ctx_len, and free sentence embedding.</sub>

> Llama3-Chinese is a large language model based on Meta-Llama-3-8B, trained using DORA and LORA+ methods on 500k high-quality Chinese multi-turn SFT data, 100k English multi-turn SFT data, and 2k single-turn self-cognition data. This model focuses on Chinese multi-turn dialogue, suitable for applications like intelligent customer service and educational tutoring, demonstrating strong language understanding and generation capabilities.

> `#Speech Recognition` `#Video Clipping` `#Large Language Model` `#Speaker Identification` `#ai-tools` `#ai-video-editing` `#asr` `#auto-subtitles` `#chinese` `#content-creation` `#funasr` `#funclip` `#gradio` `#llm` `#paraformer` `#speech-recognition` `#speech-to-text` `#subtitles-generator` `#transcription` `#video-editing` `#video-processing` `#video-subtitles` `#video-transcription` `#whisper-alternative` 
> FunClip is an open-source, accurate, and user-friendly video speech recognition and clipping tool powered by Alibaba's FunASR models. It enables precise ASR with timestamp prediction, supports LLM-based intelligent clipping via Qwen/GPT, allows speaker-specific trimming using CAM++, and offers hotword customization. With Gradio interface, it supports multi-segment clipping, SRT subtitle export, and local/server deployment for both Chinese and English content.
> <sub>FunASR-powered video transcription, subtitle generation, and LLM-assisted clipping tool with a local Gradio UI.</sub>

> Heimdallr is a lightweight notification gateway that aggregates various push channels and features Serverless deployment for nearly zero-cost operation. It is compatible with multiple notification services such as Bark and WeChat Work, offering flexible configuration options and rich functionalities, ideal for developers and teams needing efficient notification delivery. Users can quickly get started with its configuration generator, addressing webhook integration issues.
> <sub>дёҖдёӘйқһеёёиҪ»йҮҸзҡ„йҖҡзҹҘзҪ‘е…іпјҢеҸҜд»ҘиҒҡеҗҲеҗ„з§ҚжҺЁйҖҒжё йҒ“пјҢдҪҝз”Ё Serverless йғЁзҪІпјҢеҮ д№Һйӣ¶жҲҗжң¬иҝҗиЎҢгҖӮ</sub>

## huggingface/parler-tts

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 5,583 В· Python В· 2024-12-10  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/huggingface/parler-tts)  
> `#Text-to-Speech` `#Open-source Model` `#High-Quality Speech` 
> Parler-TTS is a lightweight text-to-speech (TTS) model capable of generating high-quality, natural-sounding speech tailored to specific speaker characteristics such as gender, pitch, and speaking style. This fully open-source project provides all datasets, preprocessing, training code, and weights, enabling the community to develop their own TTS models. The latest release includes two checkpoints, an 880M and a 2.3B parameter model, optimized for faster generation, suitable for various applications.
> <sub>Inference and training library for high-quality TTS models.</sub>

> GPT-SoVITS is a powerful few-shot voice conversion and text-to-speech (TTS) system that enables high-quality voice cloning with just 1 minute of training data. It supports zero-shot and few-shot inference, cross-lingual synthesis (Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, Cantonese), and includes a WebUI with tools for voice separation, dataset segmentation, and ASR. With fast inference (RTF as low as 0.014 on RTX 4090), it's ideal for personalized voice applications.
> <sub>1 min voice data can also be used to train a good TTS model! (few shot voice cloning)</sub>

The library is designed to be simple with minimal dependencies, offering an easy-to-use API for quick integration by developers. Its core features include text recognition, object detection, and slider CAPTCHA handling, with support for GPU acceleration and custom model imports to meet diverse application needs.

> KPHP is a PHP compiler that compiles a limited subset of PHP into a native binary, running 3-10 times faster than PHP. It focuses on performance and safety through whole code analysis and optimizations, supporting strict type checks and compile-time optimizations. Although KPHP has some limitations, such as not supporting certain PHP features, it offers an effective solution for developers needing high-performance PHP applications.
> <sub>KPHP вҖ” a PHP compiler</sub>

> KPHP is a PHP compiler that compiles a limited subset of PHP code into native binaries, achieving 3-10 times faster performance than traditional PHP. Developed by VK.com and open-sourced in 2020, KPHP focuses on performance and safety optimizations, supporting type inference and compile-time checks. While it does not support all PHP features, it offers an effective solution for high-performance PHP applications.
> <sub>KPHP вҖ” a PHP compiler</sub>

> Go-Crontab is a task scheduler manager based on Golang, specifically designed for the Windows environment, addressing the lack of effective scheduling tools. It supports both second-level and minute-level task scheduling, automatically recognizing the operating system to execute the corresponding commands. Users can define multiple scheduled tasks through a custom JSON configuration file, with automatic log generation for execution tracking, and it features cross-platform compatibility for Windows, Linux, and MacOS.

The plugin excels in handling large dependencies, reducing installation time from 288 seconds to just 26 seconds, greatly enhancing development efficiency. It's important to note that Composer 2 already offers improved installation speed, so users are encouraged to upgrade Composer for better performance.

> Laravel Enum is a simple, extensible, and powerful enumeration implementation designed for the Laravel framework. It supports enum key-value pairs, type hinting, attribute casting, and localization, making it suitable for applications requiring enumeration types. However, the library is no longer recommended for new projects as PHP 8.1 offers native enum support, prompting users to consider migration to the native solution.
> <sub>Simple, extensible and powerful enumeration implementation for Laravel.</sub>

## skorokithakis/shortuuid

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 2,190 В· Python В· 2026-06-20  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/skorokithakis/shortuuid) В· [Website](http://www.stavros.io/)  
> `#UUID Generation` `#URL Safe` `#Custom Alphabet` 
> shortuuid is a simple Python library that generates concise, unambiguous, and URL-safe UUIDs. It translates UUIDs to base57 encoding, avoiding similar-looking characters, making it suitable for non-sequential IDs displayed to users. The library supports custom alphabets and length settings, offering various generation methods, including name-based UUIDs and cryptographically secure random strings, ideal for diverse application scenarios.
> <sub>A generator library for concise, unambiguous and URL-safe UUIDs.</sub>

д»…ж”ҜжҢҒиӢұж–ҮгҖҒдәәж°‘ж—ҘжҠҘиҜӯж–ҷеӨ„зҗҶе·Ҙе…·йӣҶгҖҒдёҖдәӣе…ідәҺиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖзҡ„еҹәжң¬жЁЎеһӢгҖҒеҹәдәҺ14WжӯҢжӣІзҹҘиҜҶеә“зҡ„й—®зӯ”е°қиҜ•--еҠҹиғҪеҢ…жӢ¬жӯҢиҜҚжҺҘйҫҷandе·ІзҹҘжӯҢиҜҚжүҫжӯҢжӣІд»ҘеҸҠжӯҢжӣІжӯҢжүӢжӯҢиҜҚдёүи§’е…ізі»зҡ„й—®зӯ”гҖҒеҹәдәҺSiamese bilstmжЁЎеһӢзҡ„зӣёдјјеҸҘеӯҗеҲӨе®ҡжЁЎеһӢе№¶жҸҗдҫӣи®ӯз»ғж•°жҚ®йӣҶе’ҢжөӢиҜ•ж•°жҚ®йӣҶгҖҒз”ЁTransformerзј–и§Јз ҒжЁЎеһӢе®һзҺ°зҡ„ж №жҚ®Hacker Newsж–Үз« ж ҮйўҳиҮӘеҠЁз”ҹжҲҗиҜ„и®әгҖҒз”ЁBERTиҝӣиЎҢеәҸеҲ—ж Үи®°е’Ңж–Үжң¬еҲҶзұ»зҡ„жЁЎжқҝд»Јз ҒгҖҒLitBankпјҡNLPж•°жҚ®йӣҶвҖ”вҖ”ж”ҜжҢҒиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖеӨ„зҗҶе’Ңи®Ўз®—дәәж–ҮеӯҰз§‘д»»еҠЎзҡ„100йғЁеёҰж Үи®°иӢұж–Үе°ҸиҜҙиҜӯж–ҷгҖҒзҷҫеәҰејҖжәҗзҡ„еҹәеҮҶдҝЎжҒҜжҠҪеҸ–зі»з»ҹгҖҒиҷҡеҒҮж–°й—»ж•°жҚ®йӣҶгҖҒFacebook: LAMAиҜӯиЁҖжЁЎеһӢеҲҶжһҗпјҢжҸҗдҫӣTransformer-XL/BERT/ELMo/GPTйў„и®ӯз»ғиҜӯиЁҖжЁЎеһӢзҡ„з»ҹдёҖи®ҝй—®жҺҘеҸЈгҖҒCommonsenseQAпјҡйқўеҗ‘еёёиҜҶзҡ„иӢұж–ҮQAжҢ‘жҲҳгҖҒдёӯж–ҮзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұиө„ж–ҷгҖҒж•°жҚ®еҸҠе·Ҙе…·гҖҒеҗ„еӨ§е…¬еҸёеҶ…йғЁйҮҢеӨ§зүӣеҲҶдә«зҡ„жҠҖжңҜж–ҮжЎЈ PDF жҲ–иҖ… PPTгҖҒиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖз”ҹжҲҗSQLиҜӯеҸҘпјҲиӢұж–ҮпјүгҖҒдёӯж–ҮNLPж•°жҚ®еўһејәпјҲEDAпјүе·Ҙе…·гҖҒиӢұж–ҮNLPж•°жҚ®еўһејәе·Ҙе…· гҖҒеҹәдәҺеҢ»иҚҜзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұзҡ„жҷәиғҪй—®зӯ”зі»з»ҹгҖҒдә¬дёңе•Ҷе“ҒзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұгҖҒеҹәдәҺmongodbеӯҳеӮЁзҡ„еҶӣдәӢйўҶеҹҹзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұй—®зӯ”йЎ№зӣ®гҖҒеҹәдәҺиҝңзӣ‘зқЈзҡ„дёӯж–Үе…ізі»жҠҪеҸ–гҖҒиҜӯйҹіжғ…ж„ҹеҲҶжһҗгҖҒдёӯж–ҮULMFiT-жғ…ж„ҹеҲҶжһҗ-ж–Үжң¬еҲҶзұ»-иҜӯж–ҷеҸҠжЁЎеһӢгҖҒдёҖдёӘжӢҚз…§еҒҡйўҳзЁӢеәҸгҖҒдё–з•Ңеҗ„еӣҪеӨ§и§„жЁЎдәәеҗҚеә“гҖҒдёҖдёӘеҲ©з”Ёжңүи¶Јдёӯж–ҮиҜӯж–ҷеә“ и®ӯз»ғеҮәжқҘзҡ„дёӯж–ҮиҒҠеӨ©жңәеҷЁдәәгҖҒдёӯж–ҮиҒҠеӨ©жңәеҷЁдәәseqGANгҖҒзңҒеёӮеҢәй•ҮиЎҢж”ҝеҢәеҲ’ж•°жҚ®еёҰжӢјйҹіж ҮжіЁгҖҒж•ҷиӮІиЎҢдёҡж–°й—»иҜӯж–ҷеә“еҢ…еҗ«иҮӘеҠЁж–Үж‘ҳеҠҹиғҪгҖҒејҖж”ҫдәҶеҜ№иҜқжңәеҷЁдәә-зҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұ-иҜӯд№үзҗҶи§Ј-иҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖеӨ„зҗҶе·Ҙе…·еҸҠж•°жҚ®гҖҒдёӯж–ҮзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұпјҡеҹәдәҺзҷҫеәҰзҷҫз§‘дёӯж–ҮйЎөйқў-жҠҪеҸ–дёүе…ғз»„дҝЎжҒҜ-жһ„е»әдёӯж–ҮзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұгҖҒmasr: дёӯж–ҮиҜӯйҹіиҜҶеҲ«-жҸҗдҫӣйў„и®ӯз»ғжЁЎеһӢ-й«ҳиҜҶеҲ«зҺҮгҖҒPythonйҹійў‘ж•°жҚ®еўһе№ҝеә“гҖҒдёӯж–Үе…ЁиҜҚиҰҶзӣ–BERTеҸҠдёӨд»Ҫйҳ…иҜ»зҗҶи§Јж•°жҚ®гҖҒConvLabпјҡејҖжәҗеӨҡеҹҹз«ҜеҲ°з«ҜеҜ№иҜқзі»з»ҹе№іеҸ°гҖҒдёӯж–ҮиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖеӨ„зҗҶж•°жҚ®йӣҶгҖҒеҹәдәҺжңҖж–°зүҲжң¬rasaжҗӯе»әзҡ„еҜ№иҜқзі»з»ҹгҖҒеҹәдәҺTensorFlowе’ҢBERTзҡ„з®ЎйҒ“ејҸе®һдҪ“еҸҠе…ізі»жҠҪеҸ–гҖҒдёҖдёӘе°ҸеһӢзҡ„иҜҒеҲёзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұ/зҹҘиҜҶеә“гҖҒеӨҚзӣҳжүҖжңүNLPжҜ”иөӣзҡ„TOPж–№жЎҲгҖҒOpenCLaPпјҡеӨҡйўҶеҹҹејҖжәҗдёӯж–Үйў„и®ӯз»ғиҜӯиЁҖжЁЎеһӢд»“еә“гҖҒUERпјҡеҹәдәҺдёҚеҗҢиҜӯж–ҷ+зј–з ҒеҷЁ+зӣ®ж Үд»»еҠЎзҡ„дёӯж–Үйў„и®ӯз»ғжЁЎеһӢд»“еә“гҖҒдёӯж–ҮиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖеӨ„зҗҶеҗ‘йҮҸеҗҲйӣҶгҖҒеҹәдәҺйҮ‘иһҚ-еҸёжі•йўҶеҹҹ(е…јжңүй—ІиҒҠжҖ§иҙЁ)зҡ„иҒҠеӨ©жңәеҷЁдәәгҖҒg2pCпјҡеҹәдәҺдёҠдёӢж–Үзҡ„жұүиҜӯиҜ»йҹіиҮӘеҠЁж Үи®°жЁЎеқ—гҖҒZincbase гҖҒ 8зҜҮи®әж–ҮжўізҗҶBERTзӣёе…іжЁЎеһӢиҝӣеұ•дёҺеҸҚжҖқгҖҒDocSearchпјҡе…Қиҙ№ж–ҮжЎЈжҗңзҙўеј•ж“ҺгҖҒ LIDAпјҡиҪ»йҮҸдәӨдә’ејҸеҜ№иҜқж ҮжіЁе·Ҙе…· гҖҒaili - the fastest in-memory index in the East дёңеҚҠзҗғжңҖеҝ«е№¶еҸ‘зҙўеј• гҖҒзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұиҪҰйҹіе·ҘдҪңйЎ№зӣ®гҖҒиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖз”ҹжҲҗиө„жәҗеӨ§е…Ё гҖҒдёӯж—Ҙйҹ©еҲҶиҜҚеә“mecabзҡ„PythonжҺҘеҸЈеә“гҖҒдёӯж–Үж–Үжң¬ж‘ҳиҰҒ/е…ій”®иҜҚжҸҗеҸ–гҖҒжұүеӯ—еӯ—з¬Ұзү№еҫҒжҸҗеҸ–еҷЁ (featurizer)пјҢжҸҗеҸ–жұүеӯ—зҡ„зү№еҫҒпјҲеҸ‘йҹізү№еҫҒгҖҒеӯ—еҪўзү№еҫҒпјүз”ЁеҒҡж·ұеәҰеӯҰд№ зҡ„зү№еҫҒгҖҒдёӯж–Үз”ҹжҲҗд»»еҠЎеҹәеҮҶжөӢиҜ„ гҖҒдёӯж–Үзј©еҶҷж•°жҚ®йӣҶгҖҒдёӯж–Үд»»еҠЎеҹәеҮҶжөӢиҜ„ - д»ЈиЎЁжҖ§зҡ„ж•°жҚ®йӣҶ-еҹәеҮҶ(йў„и®ӯз»ғ)жЁЎеһӢ-иҜӯж–ҷеә“-baseline-е·Ҙе…·еҢ…-жҺ’иЎҢжҰңгҖҒPySS3пјҡйқўеҗ‘еҸҜи§ЈйҮҠAIзҡ„SS3ж–Үжң¬еҲҶзұ»еҷЁжңәеҷЁеҸҜи§ҶеҢ–е·Ҙе…· гҖҒдёӯж–ҮNLPж•°жҚ®йӣҶеҲ—иЎЁгҖҒCOPE - ж јеҫӢиҜ—зј–иҫ‘зЁӢеәҸгҖҒdoccanoпјҡеҹәдәҺзҪ‘йЎөзҡ„ејҖжәҗеҚҸеҗҢеӨҡиҜӯиЁҖж–Үжң¬ж ҮжіЁе·Ҙе…· гҖҒPreNLPпјҡиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖйў„еӨ„зҗҶеә“гҖҒз®ҖеҚ•зҡ„з®ҖеҺҶи§ЈжһҗеҷЁпјҢз”ЁжқҘд»Һз®ҖеҺҶдёӯжҸҗеҸ–е…ій”®дҝЎжҒҜгҖҒз”ЁдәҺдёӯж–Үй—ІиҒҠзҡ„GPT2жЁЎеһӢпјҡGPT2-chitchatгҖҒеҹәдәҺжЈҖзҙўиҒҠеӨ©жңәеҷЁдәәеӨҡиҪ®е“Қеә”йҖүжӢ©зӣёе…іиө„жәҗеҲ—иЎЁ(LeaderboardsгҖҒDatasetsгҖҒPapers)гҖҒ(Colab)жҠҪиұЎж–Үжң¬ж‘ҳиҰҒе®һзҺ°йӣҶй”Ұ(ж•ҷзЁӢ гҖҒиҜҚиҜӯжӢјйҹіж•°жҚ®гҖҒй«ҳж•ҲжЁЎзіҠжҗңзҙўе·Ҙе…·гҖҒNLPж•°жҚ®еўһе№ҝиө„жәҗйӣҶгҖҒеҫ®иҪҜеҜ№иҜқжңәеҷЁдәәжЎҶжһ¶ гҖҒ GitHub Typo CorpusпјҡеӨ§и§„жЁЎGitHubеӨҡиҜӯиЁҖжӢјеҶҷй”ҷиҜҜ/иҜӯжі•й”ҷиҜҜж•°жҚ®йӣҶгҖҒTextClusterпјҡзҹӯж–Үжң¬иҒҡзұ»йў„еӨ„зҗҶжЁЎеқ— Short text clusterгҖҒйқўеҗ‘иҜӯйҹіиҜҶеҲ«зҡ„дёӯж–Үж–Үжң¬и§„иҢғеҢ–гҖҒBLINKпјҡжңҖе…Ҳиҝӣзҡ„е®һдҪ“й“ҫжҺҘеә“гҖҒBertPuncпјҡеҹәдәҺBERTзҡ„жңҖе…Ҳиҝӣж ҮзӮ№дҝ®еӨҚжЁЎеһӢгҖҒTokenizerпјҡеҝ«йҖҹгҖҒеҸҜе®ҡеҲ¶зҡ„ж–Үжң¬иҜҚжқЎеҢ–еә“гҖҒдёӯж–ҮиҜӯиЁҖзҗҶи§ЈжөӢиҜ„еҹәеҮҶпјҢеҢ…жӢ¬д»ЈиЎЁжҖ§зҡ„ж•°жҚ®йӣҶгҖҒеҹәеҮҶ(йў„и®ӯз»ғ)жЁЎеһӢгҖҒиҜӯж–ҷеә“гҖҒжҺ’иЎҢжҰңгҖҒspaCy еҢ»еӯҰж–Үжң¬жҢ–жҺҳдёҺдҝЎжҒҜжҸҗеҸ– гҖҒ NLPд»»еҠЎзӨәдҫӢйЎ№зӣ®д»Јз ҒйӣҶгҖҒ pythonжӢјеҶҷжЈҖжҹҘеә“гҖҒchatbot-list - иЎҢдёҡеҶ…е…ідәҺжҷәиғҪе®ўжңҚгҖҒиҒҠеӨ©жңәеҷЁдәәзҡ„еә”з”Ёе’Ңжһ¶жһ„гҖҒз®—жі•еҲҶдә«е’Ңд»Ӣз»ҚгҖҒиҜӯйҹіиҙЁйҮҸиҜ„д»·жҢҮж Ү(MOSNet, BSSEval, STOI, PESQ, SRMR)гҖҒ з”Ё138GBиҜӯж–ҷи®ӯз»ғзҡ„жі•ж–ҮRoBERTaйў„и®ӯз»ғиҜӯиЁҖжЁЎеһӢ гҖҒBERT-NER-Pytorchпјҡдёүз§ҚдёҚеҗҢжЁЎејҸзҡ„BERTдёӯж–ҮNERе®һйӘҢгҖҒж— йҒ“иҜҚе…ё - жңүйҒ“иҜҚе…ёзҡ„е‘Ҫд»ӨиЎҢзүҲжң¬пјҢж”ҜжҢҒиӢұжұүдә’жҹҘе’ҢеңЁзәҝжҹҘиҜўгҖҒ2019е№ҙNLPдә®зӮ№еӣһйЎҫгҖҒ Chinese medical dialogue data дёӯж–ҮеҢ»з–—еҜ№иҜқж•°жҚ®йӣҶ гҖҒжңҖеҘҪзҡ„жұүеӯ—ж•°еӯ—(дёӯж–Үж•°еӯ—)-йҳҝжӢүдјҜж•°еӯ—иҪ¬жҚўе·Ҙе…·гҖҒ еҹәдәҺзҷҫз§‘зҹҘиҜҶеә“зҡ„дёӯж–ҮиҜҚиҜӯеӨҡиҜҚд№ү/д№үйЎ№иҺ·еҸ–дёҺзү№е®ҡеҸҘеӯҗиҜҚиҜӯиҜӯд№үж¶Ҳжӯ§гҖҒawesome-nlp-sentiment-analysis - жғ…ж„ҹеҲҶжһҗгҖҒжғ…з»ӘеҺҹеӣ иҜҶеҲ«гҖҒиҜ„д»·еҜ№иұЎе’ҢиҜ„д»·иҜҚжҠҪеҸ–гҖҒLineFlowпјҡйқўеҗ‘жүҖжңүж·ұеәҰеӯҰд№ жЎҶжһ¶зҡ„NLPж•°жҚ®й«ҳж•ҲеҠ иҪҪеҷЁгҖҒдёӯж–ҮеҢ»еӯҰNLPе…¬ејҖиө„жәҗж•ҙзҗҶ гҖҒMedQuADпјҡ(иӢұж–Ү)еҢ»еӯҰй—®зӯ”ж•°жҚ®йӣҶгҖҒе°ҶиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖж•°еӯ—дёІи§ЈжһҗиҪ¬жҚўдёәж•ҙж•°е’Ңжө®зӮ№ж•°гҖҒTransfer Learning in Natural Language Processing (NLP) гҖҒйқўеҗ‘иҜӯйҹіиҜҶеҲ«зҡ„дёӯж–Ү/иӢұж–ҮеҸ‘йҹіиҫһе…ёгҖҒTokenizersпјҡжіЁйҮҚжҖ§иғҪдёҺеӨҡеҠҹиғҪжҖ§зҡ„жңҖе…ҲиҝӣеҲҶиҜҚеҷЁгҖҒCLUENER з»ҶзІ’еәҰе‘ҪеҗҚе®һдҪ“иҜҶеҲ« Fine Grained Named Entity RecognitionгҖҒ еҹәдәҺBERTзҡ„дёӯж–Үе‘ҪеҗҚе®һдҪ“иҜҶеҲ«гҖҒдёӯж–Үи°ЈиЁҖж•°жҚ®еә“гҖҒNLPж•°жҚ®йӣҶ/еҹәеҮҶд»»еҠЎеӨ§еҲ—иЎЁгҖҒnlpзӣёе…ізҡ„дёҖдәӣи®әж–ҮеҸҠд»Јз Ғ, еҢ…жӢ¬дё»йўҳжЁЎеһӢгҖҒиҜҚеҗ‘йҮҸ(Word Embedding)гҖҒе‘ҪеҗҚе®һдҪ“иҜҶеҲ«(NER)гҖҒж–Үжң¬еҲҶзұ»(Text Classificatin)гҖҒж–Үжң¬з”ҹжҲҗ(Text Generation)гҖҒж–Үжң¬зӣёдјјжҖ§(Text Similarity)и®Ўз®—зӯүпјҢж¶үеҸҠеҲ°еҗ„з§ҚдёҺnlpзӣёе…ізҡ„з®—жі•пјҢеҹәдәҺkerasе’Ңtensorflow гҖҒPythonж–Үжң¬жҢ–жҺҳ/NLPе®һжҲҳзӨәдҫӢгҖҒ Blackstoneпјҡйқўеҗ‘йқһз»“жһ„еҢ–жі•еҫӢж–Үжң¬зҡ„spaCy pipelineе’ҢNLPжЁЎеһӢйҖҡиҝҮеҗҢд№үиҜҚжӣҝжҚўе®һзҺ°ж–Үжң¬вҖңеҸҳи„ёвҖқ гҖҒдёӯж–Ү йў„и®ӯз»ғ ELECTREA жЁЎеһӢ: еҹәдәҺеҜ№жҠ—еӯҰд№  pretrain Chinese Model гҖҒalbert-chinese-ner - з”Ёйў„и®ӯз»ғиҜӯиЁҖжЁЎеһӢALBERTеҒҡдёӯж–ҮNER гҖҒеҹәдәҺGPT2зҡ„зү№е®ҡдё»йўҳж–Үжң¬з”ҹжҲҗ/ж–Үжң¬еўһе№ҝгҖҒејҖжәҗйў„и®ӯз»ғиҜӯиЁҖжЁЎеһӢеҗҲйӣҶгҖҒеӨҡиҜӯиЁҖеҸҘеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ…гҖҒзј–з ҒгҖҒж Үи®°е’Ңе®һзҺ°пјҡдёҖз§ҚеҸҜжҺ§й«ҳж•Ҳзҡ„ж–Үжң¬з”ҹжҲҗж–№жі•гҖҒ иӢұж–Үи„ҸиҜқеӨ§еҲ—иЎЁ гҖҒattnvisпјҡGPT2гҖҒBERTзӯүtransformerиҜӯиЁҖжЁЎеһӢжіЁж„ҸеҠӣдәӨдә’еҸҜи§ҶеҢ–гҖҒCoVoSTпјҡFacebookеҸ‘еёғзҡ„еӨҡиҜӯз§ҚиҜӯйҹі-ж–Үжң¬зҝ»иҜ‘иҜӯж–ҷеә“пјҢеҢ…жӢ¬11з§ҚиҜӯиЁҖ(жі•иҜӯгҖҒеҫ·иҜӯгҖҒиҚ·е…°иҜӯгҖҒдҝ„иҜӯгҖҒиҘҝзҸӯзүҷиҜӯгҖҒж„ҸеӨ§еҲ©иҜӯгҖҒеңҹиҖіе…¶иҜӯгҖҒжіўж–ҜиҜӯгҖҒз‘һе…ёиҜӯгҖҒи’ҷеҸӨиҜӯе’Ңдёӯж–Ү)зҡ„иҜӯйҹігҖҒж–Үеӯ—иҪ¬еҪ•еҸҠиӢұж–ҮиҜ‘ж–ҮгҖҒJiaguиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖеӨ„зҗҶе·Ҙе…· - д»ҘBiLSTMзӯүжЁЎеһӢдёәеҹәзЎҖпјҢжҸҗдҫӣзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұе…ізі»жҠҪеҸ– дёӯж–ҮеҲҶиҜҚ иҜҚжҖ§ж ҮжіЁ е‘ҪеҗҚе®һдҪ“иҜҶеҲ« жғ…ж„ҹеҲҶжһҗ ж–°иҜҚеҸ‘зҺ° е…ій”®иҜҚ ж–Үжң¬ж‘ҳиҰҒ ж–Үжң¬иҒҡзұ»зӯүеҠҹиғҪгҖҒз”Ёunetе®һзҺ°еҜ№ж–ҮжЎЈиЎЁж јзҡ„иҮӘеҠЁжЈҖжөӢпјҢиЎЁж јйҮҚе»әгҖҒNLPдәӢд»¶жҸҗеҸ–ж–ҮзҢ®иө„жәҗеҲ—иЎЁ гҖҒ йҮ‘иһҚйўҶеҹҹиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖеӨ„зҗҶз ”з©¶иө„жәҗеӨ§еҲ—иЎЁгҖҒCLUEDatasetSearch - дёӯиӢұж–ҮNLPж•°жҚ®йӣҶпјҡжҗңзҙўжүҖжңүдёӯж–ҮNLPж•°жҚ®йӣҶпјҢйҷ„еёёз”ЁиӢұж–ҮNLPж•°жҚ®йӣҶ гҖҒmedical_NER - дёӯж–ҮеҢ»еӯҰзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұе‘ҪеҗҚе®һдҪ“иҜҶеҲ« гҖҒ(е“ҲдҪӣ)и®Іеӣ жһңжҺЁзҗҶзҡ„е…Қиҙ№д№ҰгҖҒзҹҘиҜҶеӣҫи°ұзӣёе…іеӯҰд№ иө„ж–ҷ/ж•°жҚ®йӣҶ/е·Ҙе…·иө„жәҗеӨ§еҲ—иЎЁгҖҒForteпјҡзҒөжҙ»ејәеӨ§зҡ„иҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖеӨ„зҗҶpipelineе·Ҙе…·йӣҶ гҖҒPythonеӯ—з¬ҰдёІзӣёдјјжҖ§з®—жі•еә“гҖҒPyLaiaпјҡйқўеҗ‘жүӢеҶҷж–ҮжЎЈеҲҶжһҗзҡ„ж·ұеәҰеӯҰд№ е·Ҙе…·еҢ…гҖҒTextFoolerпјҡй’ҲеҜ№ж–Үжң¬еҲҶзұ»/жҺЁзҗҶзҡ„еҜ№жҠ—ж–Үжң¬з”ҹжҲҗжЁЎеқ—гҖҒHaystackпјҡзҒөжҙ»гҖҒејәеӨ§зҡ„еҸҜжү©еұ•й—®зӯ”(QA)жЎҶжһ¶гҖҒдёӯж–Үе…ій”®зҹӯиҜӯжҠҪеҸ–е·Ҙе…·</sub>

## acmesh-official/acme.sh

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 47,169 В· Shell В· 2026-07-14  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/acmesh-official/acme.sh) В· [Website](https://acme.sh)  
> `#ACME protocol` `#SSL automation` `#Shell scripting` `#Wildcard certificates` `#acme` `#acme-challenge` `#acme-protocol` `#acme-v2` `#ash` `#bash` `#buypass` `#certbot` `#lets-encrypt` `#posix` `#posix-sh` `#shell` `#ssl` `#ssl-certificate` `#ssl-certificates` `#tls` `#tls-certificate` `#zerossl` 
> acme.sh is a pure Unix shell script ACME client for automating SSL/TLS certificate issuance and renewal. It fully implements the ACME protocol, supports ECDSA, SAN, and wildcard certificates, and runs on Bash, dash, and various OS including Linux, macOS, Windows, and BSD variants with minimal setup.
> <sub>A pure Unix shell script ACME client for SSL / TLS certificate automation</sub>

> `#Elasticsearch` `#Web Frontend` `#Data Visualization` 
> elasticsearch-head is a web front-end tool for managing and visualizing data in Elasticsearch clusters. It can be run through various methods, including a local server, Docker, or as a Chrome extension, ensuring compatibility with different Elasticsearch versions. Key features include an intuitive interface and flexible connection options, enabling users to easily monitor and manage their Elasticsearch clusters.
> <sub>A web front end for an elastic search cluster</sub>

> redis-py is the Python client for Redis, offering an efficient interface for interacting with the Redis key-value store. The library supports multiple Redis versions and provides a straightforward API for data operations. Key features include support for the RESP3 protocol and optional hiredis performance optimization, making it suitable for Python developers needing efficient data access.
> <sub>Redis Python client</sub>

## bagder/ca-bundle

> [!info]

> `#CA Certificates` `#Secure Connections` `#Mozilla` `#certificate-authorities` `#certificates` `#mozilla` `#pem` 
> This repository provides a PEM format backup of the Mozilla CA bundle, extracted and converted at regular intervals. Serving as a backup to the automated service on the curl website, users can access the latest CA certificate information to ensure secure connections. The project primarily supports applications relying on CA certificates, ensuring they can obtain the latest certificate data during updates.
> <sub>The Mozilla CA bundle extracted and converted to PEM. This repository functions as a backup to the automated service on the curl web site.</sub>

## rmax/scrapy-redis

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 5,642 В· Python В· 2026-05-19  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/rmax/scrapy-redis) В· [Website](http://scrapy-redis.readthedocs.io)  
> `#Distributed Crawling` `#Scrapy Extension` `#Redis Queue` `#JSON Data Support` `#crawler` `#distributed` `#redis` `#scrapy` 
> Scrapy-Redis is a Redis-based distributed extension for Scrapy, enabling multiple spider instances to share a single Redis queue for distributed crawling and post-processing. It offers plug-and-play components like scheduler, duplication filter, and item pipeline, ideal for large-scale multi-domain scraping. This fork adds JSON data support in Redis, allowing extraction of URL, meta, and form data to generate FormRequests automatically.
> <sub>Redis-based components for Scrapy.</sub>

## fideloper/TrustedProxy

> [!info]
> вӯҗ 7,311 В· PHP В· 2024-01-09  
> [GitHub](https://github.com/fideloper/TrustedProxy)  
> `#Laravel` `#Load Balancing` `#Reverse Proxy` 
> Laravel Trusted Proxies is a proxy package for Laravel that handles sessions when behind load balancers or other intermediaries. By setting trusted proxies, it ensures accurate URL generation, redirection, session handling, and logging when operating behind reverse proxies like Nginx or HAProxy. This package simplifies the configuration process, allowing developers to easily manage the real source of HTTP requests, ensuring application security and correctness.
> <sub>Laravel Proxy Package for handling sessions when behind load balancers or other intermediaries.</sub>

> Redis is an in-memory database that supports various data types like strings, lists, and sets, with persistence to disk. This project is a Windows port, officially supporting 64-bit systems and offering installation as a Windows Service. Although no longer actively maintained, it remains useful for scenarios requiring Redis in a Windows environment, particularly for high-performance data storage and quick access applications.
> <sub>Redis is an in-memory database that persists on disk. The data model is key-value, but many different kind of values are supported: Strings, Lists, Sets, Sorted Sets, Hashes</sub>

This library is compatible with Laravel 5 and above, providing quick access to nodes and their descendants.

> Swoole is a high-performance concurrency library for PHP, based on coroutines and employing an event-driven, asynchronous programming model that significantly enhances PHP's concurrency capabilities. It is ideal for building high-performance network services and real-time applications, such as HTTP servers and WebSocket services. The key features of Swoole include efficient coroutine scheduling and support for blocking I/O, enabling developers to easily implement concurrent tasks.
